methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Terranova is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Laurence Allen that has raised $7M to date. The target operates within a sector containing 9 known peers (1 public, 8 private), including comparable players such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The analysis relies on a single applicable valuation method: Stage-Progression. This approach applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement in the robotics sector, using the $7M total raised as a proxy for current value. This method yields an implied valuation of $18M. A Peer-Median approach was not applicable; while 11 same-sector peers exist in robotics and manufacturing, the peer set lacks sufficient depth at seed stage or adjacent stages to support reliable median benchmarking. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material bias given stage and strategy dispersion.

The triangulation, anchored to the single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $15M (low) to $20M (high), with a median of $18M. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-stage peer comparables, which limits cross-validation of the stage-progression multiple. Additional variability may stem from Terranova's specific technology positioning within the broad robotics sector and the composition and maturity of its investor base. Refinement of this range would benefit from either closer peer comparables at seed or Series A stages, or additional internal financial data.
